summary: Structural steel supply chains face significant headwinds from Section 232 tariff escalations targeting Chinese and Brazilian steel. Domestic mills (Nucor, Steel Dynamics) are operating near capacity with 8–12 week lead times. European mill capacity is absorbing some demand but with 30% premium over pre-tariff baseline. Vietnamese and Indian steel exporters are emerging as viable alternatives for Pacific Coast and Gulf projects with 45–60 day lead times. Buyers with flexibility on delivery timeline should lock alternative international suppliers now before Q3 demand surge.

suppliers.name: Nucor Steel – Fabricated Construction Products
suppliers.supply_route_description: EAF production at 25 US mills. Fabrication facilities in 10 states. Rebar, wide flange beams, HSS, plate. Lead times currently 8–12 weeks due to capacity constraints.

suppliers.name: Steel Dynamics – SDI Structural
suppliers.supply_route_description: Mini-mill EAF production. Structural shapes, flat rolled, steel fabrication. Columbia Structural Tubing subsidiary for HSS. Lead times 10–14 weeks.

suppliers.name: ArcelorMittal USA
suppliers.supply_route_description: Integrated BOF/BF steelmaking at Indiana Harbor and Burns Harbor. Plate, structural, hot rolled coil. Some semi-finished slab imported from Luxembourg parent.
suppliers.risk_notes: Luxembourg slab imports subject to EU trade uncertainty. US operations self-sufficient on structural grades. Ongoing labor negotiations at Burns Harbor through Q3.

suppliers.name: Tata Steel – International Export Division
suppliers.supply_route_description: Blast furnace production at Jamshedpur and Kalinganagar plants. Exported via Paradip and Haldia ports. Transit 30–45 days to US East/Gulf Coast. Competitive on structural grades ASTM A36 equivalent.

suppliers.name: Hoa Phat Group – Export
suppliers.supply_route_description: Vietnam's largest steel producer. Rebar and structural products via Ho Chi Minh City port. 40–55 day transit to US West Coast. Currently not subject to Section 232 tariffs.
suppliers.risk_notes: USTR reviewing Section 232 extension to Vietnamese steel producers. Decision expected Q3 2026. Book orders now to lock pre-tariff pricing.

suppliers.name: NLMK Group – International
suppliers.supply_route_description: Supply routed via NLMK Belgium operations to avoid direct Russia sanctions. Slab origin remains Russian. Routing via Ghent facility adds cost but enables compliant delivery.
suppliers.risk_notes: OFAC sanctions risk on Russian-origin steel remains elevated even via Belgian routing. Legal and compliance exposure significant. Use only with explicit legal clearance.
